zh.Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德
zh.Lavenham, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可拉文哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Richard Welling於1387年出生在英國素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德時，他的父親William Wellinge38歲，母親Elizabeth37歲。他於1412年與Elizabeth Lavenham在英國英格蘭素福爾可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1468年在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆享年81歲。

zh-hans.Lavenham, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可拉文哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Richard Welling于1387年出生在英国素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德时，他的父亲William Wellinge38岁，母亲Elizabeth37岁。他于1412年与Elizabeth Lavenham在英国英格兰素福爾可结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1468年在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: Welling
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Welwyn (Hertfordshire) or Welling in Bexley (Kent), with a suspicion of an alternative unidentified source. The Hertfordshire placename means ‘at the willow trees’, from Old English wel(i)gum, dative plural form of welig ‘willow’. The Kent placename may derive from Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’ + the placename forming suffix -ing. In some cases this name may represent a development of Llewellyn , particularly near the Welsh border.
German: habitational name from any of various places in the Rhineland called Welling or Wellingen.

variants.name: Wellings
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of Welling , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Llewellyn
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Richard Welling was born in 1387, in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, his father, William Wellinge, was 38 and his mother, Elizabeth, was 37. He married Elizabeth Lavenham in 1412, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1468, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Richard Welling est né en 1387, à Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Wellinge, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Lavenham en 1412, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1468, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Richard Welling 1387 in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Wellinge 38 und seine Mutter Elizabeth 37. Er hat 1412 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Lavenham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1468 im Alter von 81 Jahren in Lavenham, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Richard Welling è nato il nel 1387 nel Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, William Wellinge, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Lavenham nel 1412, nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1468, nel Lavenham, Suffolk, England, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Richard Wellingが1387年にイギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドで生まれた時，父William Wellingeは38歳，母Elizabethは37歳であった。1412年にイギリスイギリススフォルクでElizabeth Lavenhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1468年にイギリススフォルクラベンハムで81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Richard Welling(이)가 1387년 영국 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Wellinge(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1412년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 Elizabeth Lavenham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1468년 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Richard Welling nasceu em 1387, em Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Wellinge, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Lavenham em 1412, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1468, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 81 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Richard Welling родился в 1387 г. в местности литл валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Wellinge было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Lavenham в 1412 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1468 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Richard Welling nació en 1387, en Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Wellinge, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Lavenham en 1412, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1468, en Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 81 años.
